messages.author: Jordan
messages.text: There was some discussion about how to uniquely (and consistently) identify mesh nodes in a local area, so I built a small app to handle that. If you’re interested in standardizing node identification (for individual nodes and group callsigns) and making it easier to keep track of what’s active in your area, feel free to try it out. The goal is to provide a simple directory-style view of nodes with a consistent callsign format, so local coordination and troubleshooting is easier. I’m actively improving it and would love feedback—feature requests, bug reports, and suggestions are welcome. If there’s a workflow your community wants (groups, roles, discoverability, profile visibility, etc.), post it and I’ll take a look. (edited)
messages.content_html: There was some discussion about how to uniquely (and consistently) identify mesh nodes in a local area, so I built a small app to handle that. The goal is to provide a simple directory-style view of nodes with a consistent callsign format, so local coordination and troubleshooting is easier. I’m actively improving it and would love feedback—feature requests, bug reports, and suggestions are welcome. If there’s a workflow your community wants (groups, roles, discoverability, profile visibility, etc.), post it and I’ll take a look. (edited)

messages.author: Jordan
messages.text: In case anyone reads this and wonders, mesh-id now supports all countries.

messages.author: Jordan
messages.text: New additions I’m working on / now including: Network Packet Monitor: a lightweight view to observe and trace packets moving through the local mesh (helpful for troubleshooting routing, congestion, and “what’s actually happening” on-air/in-network). Weather Alerts: support for pushing relevant local alerts into the network so nodes/users can see warnings quickly. Custom Alert API Endpoint (JSON): a simple HTTP endpoint that accepts JSON payloads so external tools (scripts, dashboards, home automation, etc.) can trigger an alert that gets broadcast out over the mesh/network. If any of that matches what your community needs, I’d especially love input on: any preferred payload fields or formatting for the JSON API
messages.content_html: New additions I’m working on / now including: Network Packet Monitor: a lightweight view to observe and trace packets moving through the local mesh (helpful for troubleshooting routing, congestion, and “what’s actually happening” on-air/in-network). Weather Alerts: support for pushing relevant local alerts into the network so nodes/users can see warnings quickly. Custom Alert API Endpoint (JSON): a simple HTTP endpoint that accepts JSON payloads so external tools (scripts, dashboards, home automation, etc.) can trigger an alert that gets broadcast out over the mesh/network. If any of that matches what your community needs, I’d especially love input on: any preferred payload fields or formatting for the JSON API

messages.author: Jordan
messages.text: All of the above has been implemented with the addition of channel tracking (allows users to find available channels in a specific region).
messages.content_html: All of the above has been implemented with the addition of channel tracking (allows users to find available channels in a specific region).
